name: Tom Clancy's H.A.W.X. 2
post_apply_message:
Tom Clancy's H.A.W.X. 2 (2010, non-Steam) — TEST-FIRST setup. Apply AUTO-ADDS the Steam shortcut pointing at 'hawx2.exe' (the DX9 build, safest under Proton; 'hawx2_dx11.exe' is the DX11 build if you want to try it), game folder as start dir, GE-Proton 10.34, stable computed appid. Steam closes briefly to write it; stage GE first with 'gfm stage-runner'.
LAUNCH AND SEE:
- If it's a clean Uplay crack, it just runs (the shortcut bypasses the Ubisoft launcher).
- If it PROMPTS for Uplay / Ubisoft Connect, it's NOT fully cracked — the DRM's in the exe, same as Driver SF — tell me and we'll handle it then (sign-in or emulator route).
If you get missing-DLL errors, install any _Redist. If DX9 misbehaves, switch the shortcut to hawx2_dx11.exe.
notes: Built 2026-07-10 from the SD scan. Exes present: hawx2_dx11.exe (DX11), hawx2.exe (DX9), orbit/uplay.exe + orbit/ubisoftgamelauncher.exe (Ubisoft launcher shipped alongside). Tony believes it's DRM-cracked, so the shortcut points at hawx2.exe directly to bypass the launcher -- test-first. If it still demands Uplay, it's a compiled-DRM case like driver-san-francisco (escalate: Ubisoft Connect sign-in, or an owned-game Uplay emulator). Not in the gospel registry -> steam_shortcut derives a stable appid from the recipe id.
